free_text: Deuteron-induced reactions on 9Be, 10B, and 11B at low
energies
Low-energy, high-current accelerator,
General Ionex 1545, Colorado School of Mines
Energy spread of incident beam <1 keV.
Silicon surface-barrier detectors, covered
with nickel foils to stop intense flux of elastically
scattered particles from targets.
Accelerator beam energy calculated using known
resonance in 11B(p,alpha)9Be.
Data received by email, E. Cecil, 1997/6/30.
Approved by G. Laurent for E. Cecil, 1997/7/10
Converted to new date formats; BIB update.
Reference added.
Thick target, typiclly 1 mm. Plate of >99% pure
beryllium metal with polished surface.
Statistical uncertainty
Reaction updated; c.m. energy given.

Thick target, typiclly 1 mm. Plate of >99% pure
beryllium metal with polished surface.
Measured excitation function at 140 degrees(lab).
Angular distribution parameterized between energies at
at which thick-target yields were measured, and are
used to calculate the cross section from the measured
excitation function.
No information on source of uncertainties.
